items.title: French city of Nice marks 10th anniversary of terrorist attack
items.description: France marks the 10-year anniversary of the 2016 Nice Bastille Day truck attack. The attacker, Mohamed Lahouaiej Bouhlel, drove his truck into a crowd along the “promenade des Anglais" who had gathered to watch fireworks - killing 86 people. While Bouhlel was killed by police on the same night, eight people are accused of helping him with the attack. Their trial will begin on Monday in a special terrorism court in Paris. Catherine Viette takes a look back at the events of that night.

items.title: Mundial trumps Trump
items.description: ‘The Donald’ had bullied obsequious FIFA president Gianni Infantino into nixing a red card for the home team’s top scorer – an obscene subordination of the beautiful game to power politics. Leer más

items.title: Flowers and forensic teams at Bangkok bar where deadly fire killed at least 27
items.description: At least 27 people died and around 70 were injured in a bar fire in Bangkok, as police probe blocked exits and faulty wiring in the 50-year-old venue.

items.title: Kenya: Rights groups warn of growing 'goon' culture
items.description: Kenya is witnessing a growing use of hired gangs by political actors to intimidate opponents, disrupt protests and silence dissent, rights advocates warn.

items.title: Argentina cling to Messi in bid to break Europe’s World Cup grip
items.description: Reigning champions are the only non-European side left in the tournament and must overcome Switzerland to keep Lionel Messi’s dream alive. Leer más

items.title: Te Deum: Archbishop urges end to 'insults and cruelty' as Milei watches on
items.description: In what some saw as a pointed rebuke to President Javier Milei, Archbishop of Buenos Aires Jorge García Cuerva calls on leaders to “stop disparaging others, regardless of their political party,” as he urges greater “care for the poor, the sick and the disabled” in annual ‘Te Deum’ speech. Leer más

items.title: A tale of two footballs
items.description: The epic World Cup comeback against Egypt only stands to widen the gap between the squad of the two Lionels and all other institutions in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: What we know about the alleged murder of Jana Armstrong
items.description: The search for Toowoomba mother Jana Armstrong ended in tragedy. Here's what police say happened to the young mother.

items.title: Ramaphosa honors South Africa's WWI fallen in France
items.description: Ramaphosa also called for greater recognition of Black South Africans whose contributions to the war were ignored for decades, saying the country's history should reflect the sacrifice of all who served.

items.title: Watch: Royal welcome for Norway: 100,000 fans celebrate world cup heroes
items.description: Norway received a breathtaking heroes’ welcome as more than 100,000 fans packed the streets of Oslo after the team’s historic FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign

items.title: Sudan sentences RSF chief Hemedti to death: Who’s he, what’s he accused of?
items.description: Port Sudan trial of paramilitary leaders centres on atrocities in West Darfur.

items.title: June was deadliest month for Ukrainian civilians in four years, UN says
items.description: More than 293 Ukrainian civilians were killed and 1,990 wounded in Russian strikes across the country in the month of June, making it the highest toll in any single month since April 2022, the UN monitoring mission in Ukraine said Tuesday. The UN has recorded 16,431 verified civilian deaths in Ukraine since Russia's February 2022 invasion.

items.title: Community complains of toxic flammable gas leaks in Nigeria's Niger Delta
items.description: In Nigeria, residents in the Niger Delta town of Billé have taken to the streets to demand government action over catastrophic pollution. For several months, the community has complained of toxic, flammable gas leaking from the ground, contaminating water and destroying aquatic life – which the people rely upon for their livelihood. It's the latest in a long series of environmental disasters in the Niger Delta, which have largely been blamed on Western oil companies. Report by FRANCE 24 correspondent Sam Olukoya.

items.title: World Cup 2026: 'Spain are favourites', says French coach ahead of semi-final
items.description: Didier Deschamps has reiterated his view that La Roja remain the favourites to win the World Cup. Spain face France on Tuesday for the chance to add a second star to their shirt, having lifted the trophy only once before, in 2010. Meanwhile, Senegal's Football Federation held its first press conference since the sacking of head coach Pape Thiaw, shedding new light on the tensions behind his departure.

items.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian strike on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz that set the container ship ablaze and forced its crew to abandon it.

items.title: Yobe, Adamawa tighten security to curb illegal mining
items.description: The measures may not be unconnected with the allegations associating solid minerals, such as gold, with banditry and insurgency in some parts of the country The post Yobe, Adamawa tighten security to curb illegal mining appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. Leer más

items.title: As Milei’s aura fades, Argentina starts to look for a third way
items.description: With President Milei’s popularity at its lowest level in more than two years, talk of the 2027 election has taken over investor trips and political circles. The question being asked is whether it might be the moment for a third way. Leer más

items.title: EU, UK sign Gibraltar status treaty easing border checks
items.description: The EU and Britain on Tuesday signed a deal ending border checks between Gibraltar and Spain six years after the UK left the bloc. Gibraltar residents can now cross to Spain using ‌residence cards ​while Spanish citizens can cross using a government ID card.

items.title: Zimbabweans return home from South Africa amid anti-immigrant tensions
items.description: Zimbabweans fleeing xenophobic violence and threats in South Africa arrived at a reception centre in the border town of Beitbridge, Zimbabwe

items.title: What is Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain, the mystery site Trump warns he’ll attack?
items.description: Tensions escalate as the US president threatens to destroy Iran's fortified nuclear site in the south of Tehran.

items.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
items.description: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

items.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
items.description: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

items.title: People watching the World Cup during work hours is leading to a drop in productivity
items.description: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.

items.title: 🔴 WHO estimates DR Congo Ebola outbreak could be up to four times higher than official toll
Jurassic Park, The Piano, The Tudors... New Zealand actor Sam Neill dies at 78
items.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for his roles in "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano", has died aged 78. Neill had announced in April that he was cancer-free after undergoing treatment for stage-three ​blood cancer. Tributes are flowing in for the star, including from New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon who said Neill was "one of the greats". Story by Carys Garland.

items.title: French Embassy in Argentina slams Mendoza official's ‘racist’ remarks
items.description: Hebe Casado, Mendoza's right-wing deputy governor, defends herself after being barred from bilateral meetings with the French Embassy; Official called France's football team "ill-mannered African team" following World Cup win over Paraguay, comments French Ambassador Romain Nadal says were unambiguously racist. Leer más

items.title: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
items.description: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

items.title: Ex-navy commander convicted over 2017 ARA San Juan submarine tragedy
items.description: Submarine force commander Claudio Villamide handed three-year suspended sentence for negligence and breach of duties; Three other Navy officers acquitted. Leer más

items.title: Ted Lasso actor Fernandez makes professional debut
items.description: Ted Lasso actor Cristo Fernandez makes his professional football debut for American second-tier side El Paso Locomotive.

items.description: Argentina will meet England in Wednesday's semi-final, a day after France take on Spain in the other last-four tie. Leer más

items.title: Watch: Horses evacuated as fire approaches riding centre near Paris
items.description: The Fontainebleau forest blaze, described by officials as of 'exceptional scale', continues to rage for the second day.

items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: Meta used AI to target workers with medical conditions for layoffs, former employees' lawsuit claims
Iran war: Strait of Hormuz tanker strikes raise tensions
items.description: The UAE has threatened to retaliate after Iranian strikes on two of its oil tankers. Iran has carried out numerous strikes on US allies in response to overnight US attacks. DW has more.

items.title: Venezuela: families continue digging through the rubble for relatives
items.description: Venezuelans continue working day in and day out in the rubble to recover their relatives' bodies almost 20 days after twin earthquakes devastated the country.
